title: China-to-Egypt Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Frequency and Egypt-Specific Inverter Settings
rows.target_requirement: Egyptian grid-connected PV inverters must be configured for the local AC network and the applicable EgyptERA grid-connection code. The frequency setting should be treated as an Egypt project parameter, including permitted operating range, trip thresholds, reconnection logic, and any grid-support functions required by the network operator.
target_standards: EgyptERA medium- and high-voltage solar station connection code
rows.china_equivalent: China is also a 50 Hz market, so the nameplate frequency may look aligned. The gap is not nominal frequency alone; it is the project-specific protection settings and grid-support behavior required by Egyptian codes and the receiving network operator.
rows.gap: Do not ship only a China default firmware profile. Prepare an Egypt configuration profile, locked parameter list, commissioning record, protection-setting report, and installer instructions showing that the model can meet the approved Egyptian project settings.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected installation where EgyptERA/EETC/distribution-company connection approval specifies frequency and protection settings
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific for Chinese grid-connected installation; nominal 50 Hz compatibility does not equal Egypt project approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] 50 Hz nameplate compatibility is not enough. Egypt-specific grid-code settings and commissioning evidence should be prepared for each grid-connected project.

source.publisher: Egyptian Electric Utility and Consumer Protection Regulatory Agency (EgyptERA)

rows.requirement: China GB/T 37408 / GB/T 19964 Baseline vs Egypt Package
rows.target_requirement: An Egypt package should translate China baseline evidence into Egypt-facing evidence: EOS/GOEIC import conformity, IEC 62109 safety, EgyptERA/EETC grid-code settings, net-metering or tariff route where applicable, 50 Hz commissioning, EMC reports, English/Arabic documents where requested, and project-specific network acceptance.
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 19964 is a Chinese recommended national standard for photovoltaic power-station connection to the power system; SAMR lists GB/T 19964-2024 as current and GB/T 19964-2024 as withdrawn. GB/T 37408 is commonly used as a China PV grid-connected inverter baseline, but exporters should confirm the exact edition before citing it in the Egypt file.
rows.gap: The China baseline should become a traceability matrix, not the final conclusion. Map GB/T clauses to IEC 62109 safety, EgyptERA grid-code functions, 50 Hz protection settings, EMC reports, and GOEIC/EOS import evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Egypt-facing package is mandatory only to the extent required by import, grid-connection, project, financing, or buyer route; Chinese GB/T evidence alone is not Egyptian approval
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 19964 and GB/T 37408 are recommended GB/T baseline standards unless made mandatory by Chinese project approval, procurement, grid company, or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 37408 and GB/T 19964 are useful China baselines, but the Egypt file still needs EOS/GOEIC, IEC 62109, EgyptERA grid-code, EMC, and project-specific evidence.

source.publisher: National Public Service Platform for Standards Information / SAMR, China

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence for Solar PV Inverter Import and Project Review
rows.target_requirement: EgyptERA's renewable-energy connection document list asks for technical specifications, catalogues, and test certificates for project components. For PV inverters, EMC evidence is commonly part of the technical file, often using IEC/CISPR or equivalent standards selected by EOS, the buyer, the network operator, financier, or conformity route.
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese EMC reports may cover emissions, immunity, harmonics, flicker, and surge tests under GB/T or IEC-based methods. Check that the tested voltage, ports, enclosure rating, cable configuration, firmware, and model variants match the Egypt shipment and project configuration.
rows.gap: The gap is usually report applicability. Prepare English technical files, EMC test reports, declarations, lab accreditation evidence, and a matrix showing how each Egypt project component and model variant is covered.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by GOEIC/EOS route, EgyptERA/network-operator project review, buyer contract, or financing specification; otherwise a normal technical-file expectation
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary, project-specific, or certification-specific depending on the Chinese route and buyer requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EMC reports should be treated as model- and configuration-specific evidence for Egypt, not as generic brand-level clearance.

source.publisher: Egyptian Electric Utility and Consumer Protection Regulatory Agency (EgyptERA)

rows.requirement: EgyptERA / EETC Grid-Connection Code and Net-Metering Route
rows.target_requirement: EgyptERA publishes electricity codes including the Egyptian Transmission Grid Code, a low-voltage solar-cell project connection code, and a code for connecting solar stations on medium- and high-voltage networks. EgyptERA also publishes renewable-energy connection documents and net-metering circulars. Grid-connected inverter projects therefore need project-specific acceptance by the relevant network operator, such as EETC or the distribution company.
target_standards: Technical specification code for connecting solar cell projects to the low voltage grid
Code of technical specifications for connecting solar stations on medium and high voltage networks
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection evidence commonly cites GB/T 19964 and inverter test reports against GB/T 37408 or related NB/T rules. These documents are not a substitute for Egyptian grid-code parameter review, relay settings, anti-islanding review, metering arrangement, or local network approval.
rows.gap: The export file should include an Egypt settings sheet for voltage and frequency trip windows, reactive-power behavior, anti-islanding, reconnection, remote dispatch or curtailment where relevant, and net-metering or feed-in documentation for the actual project capacity and voltage level.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection where the inverter is installed in an Egyptian grid-connected PV project; net-metering or tariff documents are mandatory only for projects using those routes
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific for Chinese grid-connected PV projects; not transferable as Egyptian network approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese grid-code tests are a starting point only. Egypt requires project-level grid-code and metering acceptance by the relevant Egyptian network route.

source.publisher: Egyptian Electric Utility and Consumer Protection Regulatory Agency (EgyptERA)

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence for PV Power Converters
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62109-1:2010 covers general safety requirements for photovoltaic power-conversion equipment, while IEC 62109-2:2011 covers particular safety requirements for PV inverters and must be used with Part 1. For Egypt, confirm whether the applicable EOS/Egyptian Standard or project specification adopts or references these IEC standards for the inverter model.
target_standards: EOS Egyptian Standard adoption or project specification, if applicable
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese suppliers may already have IEC 62109 CB reports or GB/T inverter safety and performance reports. These reports should be checked for exact model coverage, firmware, isolation design, environmental rating, and national deviations requested by the Egyptian buyer, importer, or project owner.
rows.gap: The key gap is traceability from the Chinese test file to the Egyptian import or project requirement. Prepare a model-variant table, CB certificate and test-report package, critical-component list, and clause crosswalk to the EOS or project-specified edition.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where incorporated by EOS, GOEIC route, grid/project specification, buyer contract, or financing requirement; otherwise used as recognized safety evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Usually voluntary or customer/project driven unless required by a specific China certification, procurement, or grid-connection route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 evidence is useful but should be mapped to the exact EOS, GOEIC, buyer, financier, or project requirement before relying on it for Egypt.

rows.requirement: EOS Standards and GOEIC Import Conformity Route
rows.target_requirement: Egyptian import clearance for industrial goods can involve GOEIC services such as industrial imports, accredited tests and laboratories, import approvals, and issuance of conformity certificates. For a solar PV inverter, the importer should confirm the applicable Egyptian Standard with EOS and the GOEIC inspection or conformity route for the actual HS code, model, and shipment.
target_standards: GOEIC conformity certificates and import approvals
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin files often include GB/T inverter reports, CQC or CGC certificates, CB reports, factory ISO certificates, and Chinese labels. These can support Egyptian review but do not replace an EOS standard check or GOEIC import-conformity decision.
rows.gap: The gap is an import-route determination, not just a test-report gap. Before shipment, align HS classification, commercial invoice, model list, certificates, test reports, label language, and any inspection booking with the Egyptian importer and GOEIC route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where GOEIC import inspection, import approval, or conformity certificate is required for the shipment; no universal whole-unit PV-inverter mandatory certification is claimed here without a shipment-specific GOEIC/EOS determination
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or project/customer driven for many GB/T, CQC, CGC, and CB documents unless required by contract or Chinese project approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat GOEIC/EOS as a shipment and standard-scope gate. China certificates are supporting evidence, not an Egyptian import release by themselves.

source.publisher: General Organization for Export and Import Control (GOEIC), Egypt
